overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains an active presence across Mastodon, LinkedIn, and Bluesky, focusing on research related to social media analysis, disinformation, and computational social science. While LinkedIn attracts the largest audience, engagement varies across platforms. The recent activity indicates a focus on announcing research findings, job opportunities, and conference participation.

contentThemes.description: Announcements related to research findings, publications, and methodological discussions in computational social science.
Focus on research related to detecting and analyzing disinformation campaigns and coordinated inauthentic behavior.
Posts advertising job openings, PhD positions, and grant opportunities within the research team.
Updates regarding presentations and participation in academic conferences and events.

recommendations: Tailor content to each platform's audience. For example, share more professional updates on LinkedIn and research-specific discussions on Mastodon and Bluesky.
Increase engagement by asking more questions and prompting responses from followers.
Use relevant hashtags to increase the visibility of posts and reach a wider audience.
Consider using visuals (images or videos) to enhance the appeal of posts.
Analyze the performance of different types of content to identify what resonates most with the audience on each platform.

platformInsights.bluesky: Bluesky shows a good balance of research announcements and opportunities for engagement (PhD positions). Reposts seem to be a more prominent form of interaction on Bluesky compared to other platforms.
platformInsights.mastodon: Mastodon is used for sharing research updates and participating in methodological discussions. Engagement (likes and reposts) is relatively lower compared to other platforms.
platformInsights.linkedin: LinkedIn is primarily used for professional announcements (grants, job openings) and generates the highest engagement through likes and comments.

topPerformingContent.content: Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.

Now hiring: Two postdoctoral researchers to join our team working on computational methods for social media analysis. Background in data science, computational social science, or related fields preferred.
Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
